what_it_does: Defines a dark, vertical quick-action menu column for a virtual-hub UI kit. It includes a small inline-SVG icon helper (QmIcon), a static icon map (QM_ICONS), and a QuickMenu React component that renders icon buttons for Info, Floorplan, Share, a muted 'Gyro Not Found' item, a divider, and a Close button.
observations: Icons are built from arrays of SVG path strings passed to QmIcon.
QM_ICONS defines five icon glyphs: info, floorplan, share, gyro, close.
QuickMenu uses the active prop to add an 'active' class to the selected button.
The 'Gyro Not Found' button has className 'muted' and no click handler.
The component is exposed globally via window.QuickMenu.
Button labels and icon usage are hardcoded in the component body.
Styling is entirely className-based with no inline styles.
standouts: Custom inline-SVG icon DSL using arrays of path strings.
A static, non-interactive 'Gyro Not Found' button included in the menu.
Global window.QuickMenu assignment rather than ES module export.
conventions: Functional React components with PascalCase names.
Props named active/onSelect/onClose for controlled selection and closing.
ClassName hooks for external CSS styling.
SVG icons use currentColor for fill and viewBox '0 0 16 16'.
concerns: No accessibility attributes on buttons or SVGs (aria-label, role, title).
The muted 'Gyro Not Found' button is not marked disabled and has no handler.
Global window assignment can collide with other scripts.
notes: A compact React quick-menu with its own inline SVG icon system, meant to be styled externally and wired into a virtual-hub interface.
questions: What behavior is expected when 'Gyro Not Found' is clicked, if any?
How is the active state consumed by the parent component?
Is the global window.QuickMenu assignment required by the surrounding runtime?
What CSS rules are expected for 'vh-quickmenu', 'active', 'muted', and 'close'?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the stated purpose: the comment describes it as 'the dark menu column that pops up', and the component renders exactly such a menu.
intention_signals: Provide a compact, always-available action menu for a virtual-hub experience with consistent iconography and clear visual feedback for active and unavailable states.
novelty: The inline SVG icon factory that consumes arrays of path strings, and the inclusion of a static 'Gyro Not Found' placeholder that signals missing hardware capability.
contribution: A reusable QuickMenu React component plus a lightweight inline SVG icon system (QmIcon and QM_ICONS) that can be retained for consistent quick-action menus in virtual-hub UI kits.
summary_for_embedding: QuickMenu.jsx defines a React quick-action menu component with inline SVG icons for Info, Floorplan, Share, Gyro, and Close, driven by active, onSelect, and onClose props.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a dark pop-up menu column of icon-button actions for a virtual-hub interface.

symbols.code_id: code://counterpart-design/reference/ConceptV Design System-handoff/conceptv-design-system/project/ui_kits/virtual-hub/QuickMenu.jsx::QmIcon | component | function QmIcon({ d, fill }) {
symbols.does: Renders a 16x16 inline SVG from an array of path strings with optional fill.
symbols.description: QmIcon is a small helper component that takes an array of SVG path commands and a fill flag, producing a square svg with className 'ic' and one path element per command. It centralizes icon rendering for the quick menu.

symbols.code_id: code://counterpart-design/reference/ConceptV Design System-handoff/conceptv-design-system/project/ui_kits/virtual-hub/QuickMenu.jsx::QuickMenu | component | function QuickMenu({ active, onSelect, onClose }) {
symbols.does: Renders a dark vertical quick-menu with icon buttons for Info, Floorplan, Share, Gyro, and Close.
symbols.description: QuickMenu is a functional React component that renders a container with className 'vh-quickmenu', five buttons (one muted), a divider, and a close button. It highlights the button matching the active prop and forwards selection and closing via onSelect and onClose callbacks. It exposes itself globally as window.QuickMenu.
